view_banner_tip: ⚠️  The current v10 alpha release is unstable. We recommend using version 9.x first.
view_home_hero_title: A highly customizable lightweight financial chart
view_home_hero_desc: A professional financial charting engine for trading terminals and financial applications.
view_home_top_sponsor_desc: Empowering professionals for free, reshaping trading through innovation
view_home_quick_create_chart_title: Create a chart quickly and easily
view_home_quick_create_chart_desc: KLineChart lets you create a financial chart in just a few lines of code. It also includes a variety of commonly used technical indicators to make charts look more professional.
view_home_advantage_desc: Lightweight, professional, extensible — built for financial frontends
view_home_advantage_1_desc: Simple and fast integration with zero cost to get started. Display a chart in just 5 lines of code and focus on data integration.
view_home_advantage_1_note: A useful chart is not just one that lists many features, but one a team can integrate quickly, render immediately, and keep building on. This section focuses on low-friction adoption and a dependable first delivery experience.
view_home_advantage_1_detail_lead: The main point here is not the technical concept by itself, but whether a team can move the chart into a real page quickly. The shorter the path to first render, the cheaper it is to validate data, visuals, and interaction afterward.
view_home_advantage_1_scenario: Useful for the first integration stage, from prototype validation to production trading pages, where the team needs visible results quickly before wiring deeper data and interaction.
view_home_advantage_1_point_1: A basic chart can be integrated with only a small amount of setup code.
view_home_advantage_1_point_2: The docs, examples, and API structure make usage easier to understand quickly.
view_home_advantage_1_point_3: Works well for prototypes, internal tools, and production trading pages.
view_home_advantage_2_desc: Zero dependency and only about 50k under gzip. Charts stay smooth even with thousands of data points.
view_home_advantage_2_note: Once a finance chart enters production, it has to deal with longer time ranges, heavier data density, and repeated interaction. The goal here is a better balance between bundle footprint and runtime smoothness.
view_home_advantage_2_detail_lead: Once chart interaction starts to feel heavy, user confidence in the product drops quickly. The emphasis here is not a single performance number, but the overall sense of smoothness across real sessions.
view_home_advantage_2_scenario: A good fit for long-range historical data, repeated zoom and pan workflows, and real product sessions where rendering smoothness affects trust and usability.
view_home_advantage_2_point_1: Dependency-free and lightweight, so it is easier to adopt in existing web projects.
view_home_advantage_2_point_2: Remains stable and responsive with large datasets and frequent interaction.
view_home_advantage_2_point_3: A strong fit for finance products that care about interaction continuity and rendering performance.
view_home_advantage_3_desc: Built-in candlestick styles, coordinate axes, dozens of indicators, and drawing models to meet most needs.
view_home_advantage_3_note: The point here is not only that it can render charts, but that it already includes much of the foundation needed for professional financial interfaces. Many common requirements can start from built-in capability instead of custom work from scratch.
view_home_advantage_3_detail_lead: Feature depth determines whether the chart stays a simple rendering component or becomes a more complete finance UI capability. The stronger the built-in layer, the less duplicate work teams need during early product construction.
view_home_advantage_3_metric_1_label: chart types, indicators, and overlays ready out of the box
view_home_advantage_3_metric_2_label: supports more complete analysis and market-view needs
view_home_advantage_3_scenario: Useful for professional interfaces that need to combine market display, technical analysis, annotations, and trading-assistance information in one place.
view_home_advantage_3_point_1: Built-in chart styles, indicators, overlays, and axis systems cover common use cases.
view_home_advantage_3_point_2: Supports most market display, trading visualization, and analysis-assistance scenarios.
view_home_advantage_3_point_3: Suitable for growth from a basic chart module into a professional market view.
view_home_advantage_4_desc: Rich APIs, style configuration, and extensions for indicators, drawing models, and axes — combine features freely.
view_home_advantage_4_note: Many projects start with a usable chart, then quickly need custom interaction, domain-specific drawing tools, analysis workflows, and product-specific visual rules. Whether the chart foundation is easy to extend has a direct impact on long-term engineering cost.
view_home_advantage_4_detail_lead: If the chart layer only covers initial requirements, later changes often lead to repeated wrappers or full replacement. A more practical foundation should let teams keep building custom logic on top of the same core.
view_home_advantage_4_metric_2_label: across figures, indicators, styles, and interaction
view_home_advantage_4_scenario: Well suited to teams with a long-term product roadmap, or projects that need to build up internal charting capability over time.
view_home_advantage_4_point_1: Supports continued extension around indicators, overlays, figures, and axis systems.
view_home_advantage_4_point_2: Allows styles, interaction models, and feature combinations to follow product-specific needs.
view_home_advantage_4_point_3: Makes it easier to evolve from a general chart layer into an internal charting system tailored to the product.
view_home_advantage_5_desc: Specially adapted for mobile devices and fully capable of running in mobile browsers.
view_home_advantage_5_metric_2_label: runs directly in mobile browser environments
view_home_advantage_5_point_1: Supports common mobile gestures such as zooming and scrolling.
view_home_advantage_5_point_2: A good fit for H5 market pages and mobile trading terminals.
view_home_advantage_6_desc: Provides complete TypeScript declaration files for a first-class TypeScript development experience.
view_home_advantage_6_metric_2_label: better editor hints and safer refactors
view_home_advantage_6_point_1: APIs, configuration options, and extension interfaces are all typed.
view_home_advantage_6_point_2: Ready for direct adoption and customization in TypeScript projects.
view_home_case_desc: KLineChart works best as a charting foundation inside finance products, rather than as a standalone component.
view_home_case_1_desc: For professional trading terminals and market views that bring display, analysis, and interaction together. Configure panes, drawing tools, and axes to match your workflow, and plug into realtime or historical feeds with minimal friction.
view_home_case_2_desc: For finance apps and embedded chart modules where consistency, interaction polish, and bundle size all matter. Zero dependencies, mobile-friendly gestures, and a styling model that fits existing design systems.
view_home_case_3_desc: For research platforms, backtest panels, and strategy drills that need deeper customization. Extend with custom indicators and overlays, push bars from local data for replay, and build full analysis workflows around your models.
view_home_case_4_desc: For trading desks and operations monitoring that need several symbols in view at once. Combine multi-pane layouts, long-range scrolling, and realtime feeds without losing readability on a single screen.
view_home_users_desc: Below are some community projects and teams already using KLineChart in real pages and products.
view_home_sponsor_desc: Maintenance, iteration, and documentation work are sustained by ongoing sponsor support.
view_home_sponsor_lead: These sponsors help sustain project maintenance, feature iteration, and public availability.
view_home_sponsor_signal: Sponsor information is displayed on the official homepage
view_local_dev_dir_explain_extension: Some module directory that can be extended
view_customize_free_desc: Self-serve integration for a fast start
view_customize_free_features: Docs and examples ready for implementation
Best for individuals or small teams to validate quickly
view_customize_free_tip: Start with Free to validate your scenario, then upgrade support when needed.
view_customize_sponsor_desc: More stable support on top of self-serve usage
view_customize_sponsor_features: Priority issue assistance and practical guidance
Good for teams in active implementation
view_customize_sponsor_tip: If your project is in delivery stage, Sponsor usually improves collaboration efficiency.
view_customize_custom_desc: Project-based collaboration for complex business needs
view_customize_custom_features: Fit for deep integration and long-term evolution
view_customize_custom_tip: Share your project context and we can provide an initial evaluation path.
view_customize_subtitle: Choose the collaboration model that matches your current stage, from quick integration to deep customization.
view_customize_custom_only_note: The following content applies to custom projects only; Free and Sponsor plans do not include this level of project delivery.
view_customize_flow_desc: A structured process keeps communication predictable, delivery transparent, and launch risk manageable.
view_customize_flow_notes: Clarify business goals, key scenarios, and priorities.
Estimate complexity, timeline, technical boundaries, and feasible approaches.
Confirm interaction details, visual rules, and API contracts.
Deliver iteratively with integration checkpoints and risk sync.
Ship against acceptance criteria, then support launch and retrospective improvements.
view_customize_deliverables_desc: Different plans have different delivery depth, but each provides practical engineering outputs.
view_customize_deliverables_items: Runnable source code with key module notes
view_customize_sla_desc: Execution-focused response standards to keep communication efficient and issues closed.
view_customize_case_desc: Common scenarios and representative outcomes from real projects.

view_customize_case_items.title: Trading Terminal UI Customization
view_customize_case_items.desc: Reworked chart layout, theme, and interaction density for active trading desks.
view_customize_case_items.result: Core operation paths became shorter and onboarding cost dropped.

view_customize_case_items.title: Quant Research Tooling Extension
view_customize_case_items.desc: Added strategy annotation, replay analysis, and multi-indicator linkage.
view_customize_case_items.result: Research throughput improved with a more standardized review flow.

view_customize_case_items.title: Brokerage System Deep Integration
view_customize_case_items.desc: Integrated market data, trading, risk control, and permission systems into one chart layer.
view_customize_case_items.result: Frontend module reuse increased and maintenance overhead decreased.

view_customize_fit_desc: Aligning boundaries early reduces communication overhead and rework.
view_customize_fit_yes_items: Clear business goals and core scenarios
API docs or representative data samples available
Need for a maintainable long-term chart foundation
view_customize_fit_no_items: Direction remains unstable with no priority order
No baseline business constraints can be provided
Expecting full high-complexity scope in an extremely short timeline
Expecting one-shot delivery without ongoing communication
view_customize_faq_desc: These are the most common questions during early discussions.

view_customize_faq_items.a: Usually by complexity, timeline, and collaboration model; custom projects are quoted after evaluation.
Yes. Milestone-based delivery is recommended, starting with high-priority core capabilities.

view_customize_faq_items.q: Can we do follow-up customization after delivery?
view_customize_faq_items.a: Yes. Deliverables are kept modular to support future extension work.
Ownership terms can be clearly defined before implementation based on project type.

view_customize_compare_desc: Compare on the same dimensions to pick the best plan for your current stage.
view_customize_cta_desc: If your project is in planning or development, share your context first and we will suggest a practical path.
view_404_desc_1: Make sure the address is correct and the page hasn't moved.
view_404_desc_2: Please contact your KLineChart administrator if you think this is a mistake.
